2026-06-24.content: Identity-Aware Proxy (IAP) supports securing agent-to-anywhere egress for Agent Gateway. The feature generally available [GA](https://cloud.google.com/products#product-launch-stages). To learn more about IAP support for Agent Gateway, see [IAP for agents overview](https://docs.cloud.google.com/iap/docs/agent-overview).

summary.title: Agent Gateway now secured by IAP
summary.summary: Identity-Aware Proxy (IAP) now supports securing agent-to-anywhere egress for Agent Gateway. This feature is now generally available, offering enhanced security for your agent communications. You can find more details in the IAP for agents overview documentation.

2026-03-13.content:
You can configure custom OAuth clients in Identity-Aware Proxy by using the
Google Cloud console; the feature is generally available [(GA)](https://cloud.google.com/products#product-launch-stages).
You must use custom OAuth clients to do the following:

* Configure IAP for users who are outside of an organization.
* Customize the OAuth consent screen with custom branding.
* Provide default OAuth clients for inherited applications across all
  IAP-protected resources at the organization
  or project level.

For more information, see [Use custom OAuth clients with IAP](https://docs.cloud.google.com/iap/docs/custom-oauth-configuration).

summary.title: Custom OAuth clients now available in IAP
summary.summary: Identity-Aware Proxy (IAP) now supports custom OAuth clients, allowing for enhanced configuration for external users, custom branding on consent screens, and default client settings at the organization or project level. This feature is now generally available.

2025-10-28.content:
The ability to use a path wildcard in the `aud` (audience) field when using a
service account JWT to authenticate with an IAP-secured resource
is [generally available](https://cloud.google.com/products#product-launch-stages).

For more information, see
[Authenticate with a service account JWT](https://docs.cloud.google.com/iap/docs/authentication-howto#create_the_jwt).

summary.title: Path wildcard support for service account JWT authentication
summary.summary: You can now use a path wildcard in the `aud` field with service account JWTs for IAP-secured resources.

2025-09-23.content:
The ability to use a path wildcard in the `aud` (audience) field when using a service account JWT to authenticate with an IAP-secured resource is [generally available](https://cloud.google.com/products#product-launch-stages).

For more information, see [Authenticate with a service account JWT](https://cloud.google.com/iap/docs/authentication-howto#create_the_jwt)

summary.title: Path wildcard in audience field for service account JWT authentication is now generally available
summary.summary: You can now use a path wildcard for the audience field when authenticating with a service account JWT. This feature is generally available and simplifies authentication to IAP-secured resources.
